== Notes for [[Item:Q88183|Q88183]] ==

"Libo represents a link between the Julii Iuli of the early Republic, and the Julii Caesares, who flourished from the time of the Second Punic War to early Imperial times. It is not known whether Libo was descended from one of the Julii Iuli, or from a collateral branch of the family, nor whether he was an ancestor of the Caesars, although it has long been conjectured that they were his descendants." His wife appears to be unknown.

* Wikipedia: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucius_Julius_Libo English] | [https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucius_Iulius_Libo French] | [https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucius_Iulius_Libo German]

* Smith, (Sir) William, [https://archive.org/stream/dictionaryofgree02smituoft#page/778/mode/2up "L. Julius Libo"], ''Dictionary of Greek and Roman biography and mythology'' (1870), pp. 778-79.

ID: I62064

Name: Lucius Julius Caesar of Rome

Given Name: Lucius Julius

Surname: Caesar of Rome

Sex: M

_UID: 1EEB6332B06CE84D99F517CA4F027D012D76

Change Date: 26 Nov 2005

Death: Y

Father: Lucius Julius Caesar of Rome

Marriage 1 Spouse Unknown

Children

Lucius Julius Caesar of Rome

Forrás / Source:

http://wc.rootsweb.ancestry.com/cgi-bin/igm.cgi?op=GET&db=jdp-fam&id=I62064

--------------------

ID: I62065

Name: Lucius Julius Caesar of Rome

Given Name: Lucius Julius

Surname: Caesar of Rome

Sex: M

_UID: 12A594B78B083D4CAEA477BDAE3C710D0CDA

Change Date: 26 Nov 2005

Death: Y

Father: Lucius Julius Caesar of Rome

Marriage 1 Spouse Unknown

Children

Lucius Julius Caesar of Rome

Forrás / Source:

http://wc.rootsweb.ancestry.com/cgi-bin/igm.cgi?op=GET&db=jdp-fam&id=I62065
--------------------
see http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucius_Julius_Libo
--------------------
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucius_Julius_Libo

SOURCE NOTES:
http://www.american-pictures.com/genealogy/persons/per09768.htm#0

RESEARCH NOTES:
Claimed descent from Romulus, king of Rome

He was a Military Tribune.

Lucius Julius Libo
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Lucius Julius Libo was a member of the influential Julii clan. This patrician family was always of the most distinguished blood, however they had long since fallen out of the inner Roman elite. The Julii were active in politics since the Punic Wars.

Libo descended from the Julii who fled from Alba Longa to Rome. This city was destroyed by the Roman king Tullus Hostilius. His ancestors claim their descent from the goddess Venus and the first Roman king Romulus.

Libo was consul in 267 BC. His son of the same name was father to Numerius Julius Caesar, born before 300 BC. Numerius was a grandfather to Sextus Julius Caesar I.

Caesar is Latin for hairy. This surname was originally a nickname given to the family. Over time the nickname became a surname. The surname came about because the family's male members had hairy foreheads.

[Wikipedia, "Lucius Julius Libo", retrieved 12 Jul 08]
Lucius Julius Libo was a member of the influential Julii clan. This patrician family was always of the most distinguished blood, however they had long since fallen out of the inner Roman elite. The Julii were active in politics since the Punic Wars.

Libo descended from the Julii who fled from Alba Longa to Rome. This city was destroyed by the Roman king Tullus Hostilius. His ancestors claim their descent from the goddess Venus and the first Roman king Romulus.

Libo was consul in 267 BC. His son of the same name was father to Numerius Julius Caesar, born before 300 BC. Numerius was a grandfather to Sextus Julius Caesar I.

[[Category:Possibly derived from wikipedia]]
[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
